req.title: Manager creates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Tests for the Performance Review creation endpoint.

Requirement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a
performance review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be
validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
from unittest.mock import ANY

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_token(client, db):
    """Return a valid JWT / session token for a user with the MANAGER role."""
    response = client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"username": "manager@example.com", "password": "S3cur3P@ss!"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    return response.json()["access_token"]


@pytest.fixture
def employee_id(db):
    """Return the ID of an employee that exists in the test database."""
    return db.query("SELECT id FROM employees WHERE role = 'EMPLOYEE' LIMIT 1")["id"]


@pytest.fixture
def reviewer_id(db):
    """Return the manager's own employee record ID."""
    return db.query("SELECT id FROM employees WHERE email = 'manager@example.com'")["id"]


@pytest.fixture
def valid_payload(employee_id, reviewer_id):
    """Minimal *valid* payload satisfying all mandatory fields."""
    return {
        "employee_id": employee_id,
        "reviewer_id": reviewer_id,
        "review_period_start": str(date.today() - timedelta(days=180)),
        "review_period_end": str(date.today()),
        "overall_rating": 4,
        "comments": "Solid performance this half-year.",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers(manager_token):
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def post_review(client, payload, headers):
    return client.post("/api/v1/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)


# ===========================================================================
# Authentication & authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:

    def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(self, client, valid_payload):
        """No token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = post_review(client, valid_payload, headers={})
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_invalid_token_returns_401(self, client, valid_payload):
        """Malformed / expired token → 401."""
        response = post_review(
            client, valid_payload,
            headers={"Authorization": "Bearer this.is.not.valid"},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_non_manager_role_returns_403(self, client, valid_payload, db):
        """A plain employee must not be able to create a review → 403 Forbidden."""
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login",
            json={"username": "employee@example.com", "password": "S3cur3P@ss!"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        employee_token = resp.json()["access_token"]

        response = post_review(
            client, valid_payload,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_admin_role_is_also_permitted(self, client, valid_payload, db):
        """Admins (which include manager privileges) should also succeed."""
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login",
            json={"username": "admin@example.com", "password": "Adm1nP@ss!"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        admin_token = resp.json()["access_token"]

        response = post_review(
            client, valid_payload,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 201


# ===================
